articles.title: OpenAI’s president does ‘all the things,’ except answer a question
articles.summary: Greg Brockman, OpenAI's president, testified in Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI. His journal entries have become key evidence, while his evasive testimony style drew criticism.

articles.title: The creator of Roomba is back with a furry robot companion
articles.summary: Colin Angle, creator of Roomba, launches Familiar Machines & Magic with a new furry robot companion designed to autonomously interact with families in their homes.

articles.title: Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
articles.summary: Elon Musk sues Sam Altman and OpenAI, claiming the company abandoned its nonprofit mission to benefit humanity and prioritizes profits instead. The high-stakes trial began with jury selection in April 2026.

articles.title: As workers worry about AI, Nvidia’s Jensen Huang says AI is ‘creating an enormous number of jobs’
articles.summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang dismisses concerns about AI eliminating jobs, arguing instead that AI is creating numerous employment opportunities. He contradicts widespread worries about AI's negative impact on the workforce.

articles.title: OpenAI’s cozy partner Cerebras is on track for a blockbuster IPO
articles.summary: AI chip maker Cerebras is preparing for a blockbuster IPO potentially valued at $26.6 billion, supported by its deep partnership with OpenAI.

articles.title: Image AI models now drive app growth, beating chatbot upgrades
articles.summary: Image AI models drive 6.5x more app downloads than chatbot upgrades, but most fail to convert traffic into revenue.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with two million developers, raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS and Google Cloud's legacy systems.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code's $20-$200 monthly pricing has sparked backlash among developers. Goose, a free open-source alternative by Block, offers similar AI coding capabilities without subscription fees or cloud dependency.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign using AI tokens and a coding challenge. The creative recruitment stunt successfully attracted top engineering talent in a competitive market.

articles.title: GameStop offers $56 billion for eBay, struggles to explain how it'll pay for it
articles.summary: GameStop made an unsolicited $55.5 billion offer to acquire eBay, claiming it can improve performance by cutting costs and leveraging its 1,600 retail locations. The deal faces skepticism given eBay's market cap is four times larger than GameStop's.

articles.title: Ubuntu infrastructure has been down for more than a day
articles.summary: Ubuntu and Canonical servers have been offline for over 24 hours due to a sustained cross-border cyberattack. The outage prevents OS updates and normal communication, though mirror sites continue functioning.

articles.title: The most severe Linux threat to surface in years catches the world flat-footed
articles.summary: A severe Linux vulnerability granting root access to most Linux releases was publicly exploited after researchers disclosed it to the kernel team. Few distributions had patched the flaw before exploit code was released.

articles.title: Week one of the Musk v. Altman trial: What it was like in the room
articles.summary: Elon Musk sued OpenAI and Sam Altman in Oakland court, claiming his funding was meant for a nonprofit but the company became a corporation. The case could impact OpenAI's planned IPO this year.

articles.title: Tailoring AI solutions for health care needs
articles.summary: AI developers are targeting healthcare applications ranging from cancer treatment to administrative tasks, addressing financial pressures and labor shortages. However, success requires understanding healthcare's complexity, as many vendors have failed by misunderstanding the environment.

articles.title: Musk v. Altman week 1: Elon Musk says he was duped, warns AI could kill us all, and admits that xAI distills OpenAI’s models
articles.summary: In the Musk v. OpenAI trial, Elon Musk claimed he was deceived by Sam Altman and warned about AI dangers. He also admitted xAI uses OpenAI's models for training.

articles.title: AI evals are becoming the new compute bottleneck
articles.summary: AI evaluation has become a critical bottleneck in AI development, competing with compute resources as a primary constraint. Organizations struggle to scale evaluations needed for training and deploying advanced AI systems.

articles.title: Granite 4.1 LLMs: How They’re Built
articles.summary: Granite 4.1 LLMs represent advanced language models built with improved architectures and training methods. The models demonstrate enhanced performance across multiple AI tasks and applications.

articles.title: DeepInfra on Hugging Face Inference Providers 🔥
articles.summary: DeepInfra has been added as an inference provider on Hugging Face, enabling developers to access and deploy AI models more efficiently through the Hugging Face ecosystem.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses future directions in AI development and innovation. The conversation explores emerging trends and opportunities in artificial intelligence technology.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing strategies.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft released an open-source toolkit for modern agriculture, enabling farmers to leverage AI and technology to optimize crop production and sustainability.

articles.title: The latest AI news we announced in April 2026
articles.summary: Google announced its latest AI updates in April 2026, showcasing new developments and capabilities across its AI product lineup.

articles.title: Reduce friction and latency for long-running jobs with Webhooks in Gemini API
articles.summary: Google's Gemini API now supports event-driven webhooks for push-based notifications, eliminating inefficient polling and reducing friction and latency for long-running jobs.

articles.title: Celebrating 20 years of Google Translate: Fun facts, tips and new features to try
articles.summary: Google Translate celebrates its 20th anniversary, revealing fun facts about its evolution from a 2006 AI experiment to supporting nearly 250 languages today.

articles.title: Enabling a new model for healthcare with AI co-clinician
articles.summary: Researchers are developing an AI co-clinician to augment healthcare delivery. This new model aims to enhance patient care through AI collaboration with medical professionals.

articles.title: Announcing our partnership with the Republic of Korea
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with South Korea to accelerate scientific breakthroughs using advanced AI models. The collaboration aims to leverage frontier artificial intelligence technology for research innovation.

articles.title: Decoupled DiLoCo: A new frontier for resilient, distributed AI training
articles.summary: Decoupled DiLoCo introduces a new distributed AI training method that improves resilience and efficiency. It decouples local and global updates to enable better fault tolerance in large-scale model training.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #243 - GPT 5.5, DeepSeek V4, AI safety sabotage
articles.summary: This podcast episode discusses major AI developments including OpenAI's GPT-5.5 release with improved coding capabilities and safety monitoring, xAI's Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0 for real-time voice agents, and concerns about AI safety sabotage.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #242 - ChatGPT Images 2.0, Qwen 3.6 Max, Kimi-K2.6
articles.summary: OpenAI released ChatGPT Images 2.0 with improved text and screenshot generation. The episode discusses latest AI developments including Qwen 3.6 Max and Kimi-K2.6.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family, and discussions covered Mamba 3 and attention residuals.

articles.title: Physical AI raises governance questions for autonomous systems
articles.summary: Autonomous AI systems in robots and industrial equipment raise governance challenges around testing, monitoring, and control. Industrial robot installations are doubling, reaching 542,000 units in 2024 with expectations to exceed 700,000 by 2028.

articles.title: Google made agentic AI governance a product. Enterprises still have to catch up.
articles.summary: Google launched Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform at Cloud Next '26, making agentic AI governance a native product feature with built-in cryptographic identity tracking and Agent Gateway oversight for enterprise compliance.

articles.title: SAP: How enterprise AI governance secures profit margins
articles.summary: Enterprise AI governance replaces statistical guesses with deterministic control, securing profit margins. Organizations must prioritize precision and accuracy as AI moves into production, where the gap between 90% and 100% accuracy is existential.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur during problem-solving.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses localization, destination identification, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: How Memory Sparse Attention scales LLM memory to 100 million tokens
articles.summary: Memory Sparse Attention enables large language models to process 100 million token contexts while maintaining accuracy, significantly expanding LLM memory capabilities.

articles.title: Claude Code is leaking API keys into public package registries
articles.summary: A study reveals that Claude Code and similar AI coding assistants are leaking sensitive API keys to public code repositories and package registries.

articles.title: Anthropic’s MCP vulnerability: When ‘expected behavior’ becomes a supply chain nightmare
articles.summary: Security researchers discovered a critical architectural flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol that exposes millions of AI applications to remote takeovers, creating significant supply chain risks.

articles.title: Why Gradient Descent Zigzags and How Momentum Fixes It
articles.summary: Gradient descent struggles with uneven loss surfaces, zigzagging when terrain is steep in one direction and flat in another. Momentum fixes this by averaging past gradients, reinforcing consistent directions while canceling oscillations.

articles.title: Google Adds Event-Driven Webhooks to the Gemini API, Eliminating the Need for Polling in Long-Running AI Jobs
articles.summary: Google introduced event-driven webhooks for the Gemini API, replacing inefficient polling with push-based notifications for long-running AI jobs. This eliminates wasteful repeated requests and improves reliability at scale.

articles.title: A Coding Guide to Survey Bias Correction Using Facebook Research Balance with IPW CBPS Ranking and Post Stratification Methods
articles.summary: Tutorial on correcting survey bias using Facebook's balance library, demonstrating IPW, CBPS, ranking, and post-stratification methods to recover unbiased population estimates.

articles.title: Forget the soundbar: How I upgraded my TV audio with spare Bluetooth speakers
articles.summary: Users can upgrade TV audio using spare Bluetooth speakers instead of expensive soundbars or speaker systems. This budget-friendly alternative provides robust sound without major financial investment.

articles.title: Android phone slow? I changed 2 developer settings for an instant speed boost
articles.summary: Enable Android Developer Options to boost phone performance without using optimizer apps. Two simple settings changes can instantly speed up your device.

articles.title: This wearable gadget effectively soothes my migraines and headaches, and it's under $50
articles.summary: The Renpho Eyeris 2 Massager is an affordable wearable device under $50 that alleviates headaches and eye strain while enabling music streaming.

articles.title: Agentic RAG Explained in 3 Levels of Difficulty
articles.summary: Agentic RAG combines autonomous agents with retrieval-augmented generation, enabling AI systems to independently decide when and how to retrieve information for improved decision-making and accuracy.

articles.title: Effective KV Compression with TurboQuant
articles.summary: Google launched TurboQuant, a new algorithmic suite for quantizing and compressing large language models and vector search engines, essential for RAG systems.

articles.title: Building AI Agents in Python with Pydantic AI
articles.summary: Learn to build AI agents using Python and Pydantic AI framework. This guide covers practical implementation techniques for creating intelligent autonomous agents.

articles.title: Single Agent vs Multi-Agent: When to Build a Multi-Agent System
articles.summary: Guide comparing single-agent and multi-agent AI systems, exploring ReAct workflows and when to scale from one agent to multiple agents for improved performance.

articles.title: How to Build an Efficient Knowledge Base for AI Models
articles.summary: Building an efficient knowledge base for AI models requires continuous iteration and refinement rather than a one-time effort. The process involves systematic improvement of data quality and organization.

articles.title: Playing Connect Four with Deep Q-Learning
articles.summary: Deep Q-Learning is applied to solve Connect Four, a multiplayer game, using function approximation techniques to train an AI agent.

articles.title: Nemotron Labs: What OpenClaw Agents Mean for Every Organization
articles.summary: OpenClaw agents have become a major open-source phenomenon with over 100,000 GitHub stars by early 2026, enabling organizations to build specialized AI systems on NVIDIA platforms with transparency and scalability.

articles.title: It’s Gonna Be May: 16 Games Hit the Cloud This Month, With More NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5080 Power
articles.summary: 16 games are joining NVIDIA GeForce NOW this May, including AAA titles like Forza Horizon 6 and 007 First Light. RTX 5080 power expands to the Install-to-Play library for enhanced streaming across devices.

articles.title: NVIDIA Launches Nemotron 3 Nano Omni Model, Unifying Vision, Audio and Language for up to 9x More Efficient AI Agents
articles.summary: NVIDIA launched Nemotron 3 Nano Omni, a unified multimodal model combining vision, audio, and language capabilities into one system for 9x more efficient AI agents with advanced reasoning.

articles.title: Beyond BI: How the Dataset Q&A feature of Amazon Quick powers the next generation of data decisions
articles.summary: Amazon QuickSight's Dataset Q&A feature enables business leaders to ask natural language questions about data and receive instant answers without waiting for BI teams to build new dashboards, bridging the gap between static dashboards and ad-hoc data exploration.

articles.title: Introducing the agent quality loop: AgentCore Optimization now in preview
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore introduces an agent quality loop in preview, enabling teams to automatically generate recommendations from production traces, validate them through batch evaluation and A/B testing, and deploy improvements with confidence.

articles.title: Agent-guided workflows to accelerate model customization in Amazon SageMaker AI
articles.summary: Amazon SageMaker AI introduces an agentic experience that simplifies model customization by allowing developers to describe use cases in natural language. The AI agent automates the entire workflow from data preparation through fine-tuning technique selection, evaluation, and deployment.

articles.title: Optimize Supply Chain Decision Systems Using NVIDIA cuOpt Agent Skills
articles.summary: NVIDIA's cuOpt Agent Skills optimizes supply chain decisions by automating complex operations research problems, addressing challenges like fluctuating demand and volatile costs.

articles.title: Speed Up Unreal Engine NNE Inference with NVIDIA TensorRT for RTX Runtime
articles.summary: NVIDIA TensorRT optimizes neural network inference in Unreal Engine's NNE for RTX, accelerating graphics tasks like super resolution and denoising for improved performance and image quality.

articles.title: Build AI-Powered Games with NVIDIA DLSS 4.5, RTX, and Unreal Engine 5
articles.summary: NVIDIA releases DLSS 4.5 with Dynamic Multi Frame Generation and advanced Super Resolution for game developers using Unreal Engine 5, enabling AI-powered graphics optimization.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study explores how different generations perceive social media advertisements across various platforms, examining their distinct attitudes toward ads.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta committed to achieving net zero emissions by 2030, developing carbon mitigation technology and sharing it openly to reduce its environmental impact.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with an unconventional background pioneered innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experiences can drive breakthrough advancements in cutting-edge tech development.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a gradient-based planner for world models enabling practical long-horizon planning through virtual state lifting, stochastic exploration, and gradient reshaping to improve optimization stability.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers present methods for identifying interactions at scale in Large Language Models to improve interpretability. The work aims to make LLM decision-making more transparent for safer and more trustworthy AI systems.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven design of imaging systems that optimizes optical systems to create measurements distinguishing objects. Uses encoders and noise models to quantify measurement effectiveness.
